explanation: Tableau connects to spreadsheets, databases, and cloud services. The Data Source page configures connections, joins, and unions. Tableau Prep handles complex cleaning workflows.
example:
```
# Tableau Workflow (design tool)

1. CONNECT TO DATA
   - File → New → Connect to: Excel, CSV, PostgreSQL, Google Sheets
   - Drag tables to canvas
   - Define joins: Inner, Left, Right, Full Outer

2. DATA SOURCE CONFIGURATION
   - Relationship lines between matching fields
   - Custom SQL for complex queries
   - Union: stack tables with matching columns

3. EXTRACT vs LIVE
   - Live: queries run against source
   - Extract: snapshot (faster, offline)
   - Incremental refresh for large datasets

4. DATA TYPES
   - String, Number (decimal/integer), Date, Boolean, Geographic

5. FIELD CALCULATIONS
   - Create Calculated Field: [Profit Ratio] = SUM([Profit]) / SUM([Sales])
   - Table Calculations: running total, percent of total
   - Level of Detail (LOD): FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE
```
key_takeaway: Tableau connects to any data source. Use joins for related tables, unions for stacking. Extracts are faster than live connections.
try_it: Connect Tableau to a CSV, create a calculated field, and build a bar chart showing the metric by category.

explanation: Worksheets are where visualizations live. Drag fields to shelves: Columns (x-axis), Rows (y-axis), Color, Size, Label. The Marks card controls how data points appear — bar, line, circle, map.
example:
```
# Building Visualizations

1. CREATE WORKSHEET
   - Drag fields to shelves:
     * Columns → x-axis
     * Rows → y-axis
     * Color → encodes by color
     * Size → encodes by size
     * Label → shows values

2. MARK TYPES
   - Bar: categorical comparisons
   - Line: trends over time
   - Circle: scatter plots
   - Map: geographic data
   - Text: crosstabs

3. TABLE CALCULATIONS
   - Running Total: cumulative sum
   - Percent of Total: each value as % of total
   - Moving Average: smoothed trend

4. FILTERS
   - Drag to Filters shelf
   - Categorical, Range, Relative Date, Top N
   - Show Filter: interactive control

5. PARAMETERS
   - User-input variable
   - Use in calculations: [Sales] * [Growth Rate Parameter]
```
key_takeaway: Drag fields to shelves to build charts. Marks card controls visual encoding. Table calculations compute on the visualization.
try_it: Create a bar chart showing sales by category with color for profit ratio and a Top N filter.

explanation: Calculated fields create new data from existing fields using formulas. Range from simple arithmetic to complex conditional logic. LOD expressions control aggregation granularity.
example:
```
# Calculated Field Formulas

# Basic
[Profit Margin] = [Profit] / [Sales]

# Conditional
[Customer Tier] = IF [Total] >= 10000 THEN "Platinum"
                  ELSEIF [Total] >= 5000 THEN "Gold"
                  ELSE "Silver" END

# Date
[Days Since] = DATEDIFF('day', [Order Date], TODAY())

# LOD
[Customer First Order] = { FIXED [Customer ID] : MIN([Order Date]) }
[Avg by Category] = { FIXED [Category] : AVG([Sales]) }

# Table calculations
[Running Total] = RUNNING_SUM(SUM([Sales]))
[Pct of Total] = SUM([Sales]) / TOTAL(SUM([Sales]))
[YoY Growth] = (SUM([Sales]) - LOOKUP(SUM([Sales]), -4)) / ABS(LOOKUP(SUM([Sales]), -4))
```
key_takeaway: Calculated fields create metrics. LOD expressions (FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE) control aggregation. Table calculations (LOOKUP, RUNNING_SUM) compute relative to visualization.
try_it: Create a customer tier classification and visualize the distribution.

explanation: Dashboards combine worksheets with interactive actions. Filter actions let users click one chart to filter all others. Stories sequence dashboards into narratives.
example:
```
# Dashboard Creation

1. CREATE DASHBOARD
   - New Dashboard → set size (1200x800)
   - Drag worksheets onto canvas

2. LAYOUT
   - Horizontal/Vertical containers
   - Tiled (fixed) or Floating (overlapping)

3. ACTIONS
   - Filter: click chart A to filter chart B
   - Highlight: highlight related marks
   - URL: open external links
   - Parameter: change parameter on click

4. FILTERS
   - Show Filter: interactive dropdown/slider
   - Apply to: this sheet, selected sheets, all

5. STORIES
   - Sequenced dashboards for presentations
   - Add captions and annotations

6. MOBILE
   - Device Layout → Phone
   - Rearrange for mobile view
```
key_takeaway: Dashboards combine worksheets with interactive filter actions. Use containers for layout. Filter actions let users click one chart to filter all others.
try_it: Create a dashboard with 3 charts and a filter action so clicking a category filters the others.

explanation: Tableau Server/Cloud hosts dashboards for team access. Published dashboards are interactive in a browser. Schedules keep extracts fresh. Permissions control access.
example:
```
# Publishing

1. PUBLISH
   - Server → Publish Workbook
   - Select project, set permissions

2. DATA REFRESH
   - Schedule: hourly, daily, weekly
   - Incremental: only new rows

3. PERMISSIONS
   - Project: inherit or custom
   - Workbook: view, interact, download, edit

4. SUBSCRIPTIONS
   - Email snapshot: daily/weekly

5. EMBEDDING
   - iframe in web pages
   - JavaScript API for programmatic control

6. EXPORT
   - Image (PNG), Data (CSV), PDF, PowerPoint
```
key_takeaway: Publish to Server/Cloud for team access. Schedule extract refreshes. Control permissions at project/workbook level. Embed with iframe or JavaScript API.
try_it: Publish a dashboard to Tableau Public and share the link with a colleague.

explanation:
Tableau problems test your ability to connect data, build visualizations, create calculated fields, and design interactive dashboards. The core patterns are: data connections (joins, unions, extracts), visual encoding (marks, shelves, color/size), calculated fields (LOD expressions, table calculations), and dashboard design (filter actions, parameters, layout). Understanding when to use different chart types and how to create effective data stories is essential.

Interview questions focus on building calculated fields with LOD expressions, designing dashboards with interactive filter actions, and optimizing data sources with extracts. Production Tableau adds challenges like performance optimization (extracts vs live), row-level security, and automated refresh scheduling.
key_takeaway: Tableau problems follow: connect data, build worksheets with the right chart types, add calculated fields for metrics, and compose dashboards with interactive actions. LOD expressions (FIXED, INCLUDE, EXCLUDE) control aggregation granularity.
try_it: Build a dashboard with 3 charts connected by filter actions. Add a parameter to switch between different metrics.

practice_challenges.title: Create Customer Segmentation
practice_challenges.hint: Use IF/ELSEIF with SUM([Revenue]) thresholds to classify customers
practice_challenges.description: Build a calculated field that segments customers into tiers (Platinum, Gold, Silver, Bronze) based on their total purchase amount. Create a bar chart showing the distribution of customers per tier.
practice_challenges.expected_output: Customer tier calculated field and bar chart showing count per tier

practice_challenges.title: Build a Sales Dashboard
practice_challenges.hint: Combine 3 worksheets in a horizontal layout container, add filter actions
practice_challenges.description: Create a dashboard with 3 charts: sales by category (bar), sales over time (line), and top 10 products (table). Add a filter action so clicking a category filters the other two charts.
practice_challenges.expected_output: Interactive dashboard where clicking a category updates all charts

practice_challenges.title: Implement LOD Expressions
practice_challenges.hint: FIXED [dimension] : AGG([measure]) computes at a specific granularity regardless of viz level
practice_challenges.description: Create LOD expressions to: 1) find each customer's first order date, 2) compute category-level average sales, 3) calculate the difference between each sale and its category average. Visualize the results.
practice_challenges.expected_output: Three LOD-calculated fields with visualizations showing their values

practice_challenges.title: Build Table Calculations for YoY Analysis
practice_challenges.hint: LOOKUP(SUM([Sales]), -12) references the value 12 periods ago
practice_challenges.description: Create year-over-year growth calculations using LOOKUP and RUNNING_SUM. Build a dual-axis chart showing actual sales and YoY growth percentage. Add a moving average trend line.
practice_challenges.expected_output: Dual-axis chart with sales, YoY growth %, and 3-month moving average

practice_challenges.title: Design an Executive Dashboard with Parameters
practice_challenges.hint: Create a parameter for metric selection, use CASE in a calculated field to switch between measures
practice_challenges.description: Build an executive dashboard with: a parameter to switch between Revenue, Profit, and Quantity metrics, KPI cards with color-coded indicators, a trend line, and a geographic map. Add device-specific layouts for mobile.
practice_challenges.expected_output: Executive dashboard with metric-switching parameter, KPI cards, and mobile layout
